Saw a few people show off custom GitHub profile pages recently, so I figured I'd write down how to do it.
First step — create a repo that has the
When you create one with a matching name, GitHub shows you this banner:
You found a secret! hsiangfeng/hsiangfeng is a ✨special ✨ repository that you can use to add a README.md to your GitHub profile. Make sure it's public and initialize it with a README to get started.
". Whatever README.md you put in this repo gets displayed on your GitHub profile page. If you don't want to build a personal landing page, this is a pretty nice middle ground.
Clone the repo and add a README.md. Just Markdown — write whatever you want, push it up, and the description shows up on your profile.
I wanted my profile to show stats from my repos too, so I found
github-readme-stats is built for the profile repo use case. It generates dynamic SVG stats cards. Usage is super simple — replace
language-md shiki shiki-themes github-light github-light github-dark-high-contrast
Push and the card shows up on your profile:
. Adding a theme is just one extra param
. I like Vue's color palette, so I went with:
Beyond the stats card above, you can also show a language breakdown for your repos. Same pattern — just swap
makes it denser. Themes work the same way.

Seen people show off their GitHub profile pages and wondered how to do that? This post is the quick tour — creating the special username-named repo with a README that becomes your profile, plus using the github-readme-stats project to add stats cards, theme them, and show your top languages.
